heading: Regulations by President as to commissions, fees of witnesses, counsel and interpreters
label: 3496 Regulations by President as to commissions, fees of witnesses, counsel and interpreters
text: The President is authorized to prescribe regulations governing the manner of executing and returning commissions by consular officers under the provisions of sections 3492–3494 of this title and schedules of fees allowable to witnesses, foreign counsel, and interpreters under .section 3495 of this title
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on , U.S.C., 1940 ed., Judicial Code and Judiciary (, ).section 695g of title 28June 20, 1936, ch. 640, § 849 Stat. 1564

Ex. Ord. No. 10307. Delegation of AuthorityEx. Ord. No. 10307, , 16 F.R. 11907, provided:Nov. 23, 1951By virtue of the authority vested in me by the act of , (3 U.S.C. Supp. 301–303), I hereby delegate to the Secretary of State (1) the authority vested in the President by of the United States Code () to prescribe regulations governing the manner of executing and returning commissions by consular officers under the provisions of sections 3492–3494 of the said title, and schedules of fees allowable to witnesses, foreign counsel, and interpreters under section 3495 of the said title, and (2) the authority vested in the President by of the United States Code () to prescribe regulations making the provisions of sections 3492–3496 of the said title applicable to diplomatic officers.August 8, 195064 Stat. 419section 3496 of title 1862 Stat. 836section 3492(c) of title 1862 Stat. 835Executive Order No. 8298 of , entitled “Regulations Governing the Manner of Executing and Returning Commissions by Officers of the Foreign Service in Criminal Cases, and Schedule of Fees and Compensation in Such Cases”, is hereby revoked.December 4, 1939
